sutta: once in the gable hall at veithali maha pajapati the gotami spoke thus to the exalted 1 well word for me lord if the exalted 1 would teach me dhamma briefly so that after hearing the exalted 1's word i might dwell alone secluded earnest zealous and resolute and the buddha said those things of which you know thus these things lead to passion not to release there from these to lead to bondage not to release there from these lead to the piling of rebirth not to the dispersion thereof these lead to wanting much not wanting little these lead to discontent not to contentment these lead to sociability not to solitude these lead to indolence not to exertion these lead to luxury not to frugality of these things oh definitely this is not dhamma this is not vinaya this is not the word of the teacher but as to those things gotami which you know lead to this passion to release from bondage to the dispersion of rebirth to wanting little to contentment to solitude to exertion and to frugality and in no case to the opposites be assured that they are dhamma they are vinaya the word of the teacher that's the end of the suta
commentary: so here the buddha is giving a dharma teaching in brief to the foster mother to the mother and uh these are the things that uh there is dharma in a concise form that those things that lead to dispassion to release from bondage to dispersion of rebirth to wanting little to contentment to solitude to exertion to frugality these are consistent with the dhamma vinaya but not their opposite the nexus
